title: C Pointers and Arrays
content:
### Pointers and Arrays in C

Arrays and pointers are closely related in C. The array name acts as a pointer to the first element.

### Array Name as Pointer

```c
int arr[5] = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50};
int *p = arr;  // equivalent to int *p = &arr[0]
```

### Equivalence

| Array Notation | Pointer Notation |
|----------------|-----------------|
| `arr[0]` | `*arr` |
| `arr[i]` | `*(arr + i)` |
| `&arr[i]` | `arr + i` |
| `arr` | `&arr[0]` |

### Key Difference

| Aspect | Array | Pointer |
|--------|-------|---------|
| Declaration | `int arr[5]` | `int *p` |
| sizeof | Total array bytes | Pointer size (4 or 8) |
| Assignment | Cannot be reassigned | Can point elsewhere |
| Memory | Fixed block on stack | Can point to heap or stack |

### Pointer to Array vs Array of Pointers

```c
int (*ptr)[5];  // pointer to an array of 5 ints
int *ptr[5];    // array of 5 int pointers
```

---

> **Key Point:** `arr[i]` is equivalent to `*(arr + i)`. The array name cannot be reassigned, but a pointer can.
